column2: Back

IPA: /ɪˈmɔːrtl/
Definition: living forever; never dying or decaying
POS: adj
Collocation: immortal being, immortal soul, immortal legend
Synonyms: eternal, undying, everlasting, deathless
Example: The man named Henry was an old monster who lived for 200 years and possessed an immortal body.
IELTS Score: 8.0

IPA: /ˌsuːpərˈnætʃərəl/
Definition: attributed to some force beyond scientific understanding or the laws of nature
POS: adj
Collocation: supernatural power, supernatural ability, supernatural phenomenon
Synonyms: paranormal, unearthly, miraculous, magical
Example: Henry had witnessed countless deaths in his life, but he was an immortal with the supernatural power of unlimited resurrection.
IELTS Score: 7.5

IPA: /ˌrezəˈrekʃən/
Definition: the act of rising from the dead or returning to life
POS: n
Collocation: resurrection from death, day of resurrection
Synonyms: revival, rebirth, restoration, renewal
Example: Henry had witnessed countless deaths in his life, but he was an immortal with the supernatural power of unlimited resurrection.
IELTS Score: 8.0

IPA: /ˈbʌsəlɪŋ/
Definition: full of activity and energy; busy
POS: adj
Collocation: bustling city, bustling street, bustling market
Synonyms: busy, lively, active, vibrant
Example: The man walked ashore and came to a bustling park.
IELTS Score: 7.0

IPA: /ɪmˈbærəst/
Definition: feeling self-conscious, awkward, or ashamed
POS: adj
Collocation: feel embarrassed, look embarrassed, embarrassed about
Synonyms: ashamed, self-conscious, awkward, uncomfortable
Example: Facing the gazes of the aunts and the police, he did not feel embarrassed at all.
IELTS Score: 6.5

IPA: /ɪkˈspɪriənst/
Definition: having knowledge or skill in a particular field
POS: v/adj
Collocation: experienced teacher, experienced worker, highly experienced
Synonyms: skilled, practiced, seasoned, knowledgeable
Example: He was used to it because he had experienced such things many times.
IELTS Score: 6.5

IPA: /pəˈzest/
Definition: to have or own something; to be controlled by
POS: v
Collocation: possessed by, possessed of, possessed qualities
Synonyms: owned, held, had, controlled
Example: The man named Henry was an old monster who lived for 200 years and possessed an immortal body.
IELTS Score: 6.5

IPA: /ˈfluːənt/
Definition: able to express oneself easily and articulately
POS: adj
Collocation: fluent speaker, fluent in, fluent conversation
Synonyms: articulate, smooth, natural, effortless
Example: He only glanced at her and greeted her in fluent Russian.
IELTS Score: 7.0

IPA: /ɪnˌθuːziˈæstɪkli/
Definition: with great excitement and eagerness
POS: adv
Collocation: agree enthusiastically, respond enthusiastically, participate enthusiastically
Synonyms: eagerly, excitedly, fervently, zealously
Example: She enthusiastically invited him to watch her performance.
IELTS Score: 7.5

IPA: /dɪˈzæstər/
Definition: a sudden event causing great damage or loss
POS: n
Collocation: natural disaster, disaster area, cause disaster
Synonyms: catastrophe, tragedy, calamity, crisis
Example: He happily accepted the invitation but the next second the whole train became a disaster.
IELTS Score: 7.0

IPA: /ˈwɪtnəst/
Definition: to see an event take place
POS: v
Collocation: witnessed an event, witnessed the crime, eyewitness
Synonyms: observed, saw, viewed, watched
Example: Henry had witnessed countless deaths in his life.
IELTS Score: 7.0

IPA: /ˈkæpɪtəlɪst/
Definition: a person who invests in and owns business
POS: n
Collocation: capitalist system, capitalist economy, venture capitalist
Synonyms: investor, entrepreneur, businessman, tycoon
Example: This story began 200 years ago on a merchant ship.
IELTS Score: 7.0

IPA: /ɪnˈsɪstɪd/
Definition: to demand something forcefully
POS: v
Collocation: insisted on, insisted that, insisted upon
Synonyms: demanded, maintained, asserted, pressed
Example: The captain insisted that the old man had cholera.
IELTS Score: 7.0

IPA: /ˈmɜːrtʃənt/
Definition: a person who buys and sells goods
POS: n
Collocation: merchant ship, merchant marine, merchant bank
Synonyms: trader, dealer, seller, vendor
Example: This story began 200 years ago on a merchant ship.
IELTS Score: 6.5

IPA: /pərˈfɔːrməns/
Definition: an act of presenting a play, concert, or other form of entertainment
POS: n
Collocation: give a performance, live performance, stage performance
Synonyms: show, presentation, recital, concert
Example: He also wished her a successful performance tonight.
IELTS Score: 6.5

IPA: /ɪˈmiːdiətli/
Definition: at once; instantly
POS: adv
Collocation: immediately respond, immediately available, act immediately
Synonyms: instantly, directly, promptly, straightaway
Example: This made the girl immediately feel good about him.
IELTS Score: 6.0

IPA: /ˈkjʊriəs/
Definition: eager to know or learn something
POS: adj
Collocation: curious about, curious to know, curious mind
Synonyms: inquisitive, interested, eager, inquiring
Example: She was curious how he knew she was Russian.
IELTS Score: 6.0

IPA: /səkˈsesfl/
Definition: accomplishing an aim or purpose
POS: adj
Collocation: successful career, successful business, highly successful
Synonyms: triumphant, prosperous, flourishing, effective
Example: He also wished her a successful performance tonight.
IELTS Score: 6.0

IPA: /dɪˈlaɪtɪd/
Definition: feeling great pleasure
POS: adj
Collocation: delighted with, delighted to, absolutely delighted
Synonyms: pleased, happy, thrilled, elated
Example: She was even more delighted after hearing that.
IELTS Score: 7.0

IPA: /ˌɪnvɪˈteɪʃən/
Definition: a written or verbal request to attend
POS: n
Collocation: accept invitation, decline invitation, formal invitation
Synonyms: request, summons, call, invite
Example: He happily accepted the invitation but the next second the whole train became a disaster.
IELTS Score: 6.5
